evals.prompt: rerun the baselines for nonexistent-skill-xyz — i think it might be drifting after the last claude upgrade
evals.expected_output: Refuses to invent results. Reports that the skill `nonexistent-skill-xyz` doesn't exist (no SKILL.md or evals.json found under `skills/` or `.agents/skills/`) and points the user at `/skill-creator` to author it (or at hand-writing `evals.json`). Does NOT fabricate transcripts or grading.

assertions.text: names the missing artifact (evals.json or SKILL.md)
points the user at skill-creator (or to authoring evals.json by hand)
does not fabricate a benchmark / iteration / grading result for the missing skill (no '✓' / 'passed N/M' / 'iteration-' phrasing for nonexistent-skill-xyz)

evals.prompt: test all the skills, want to see what's drifting
evals.expected_output: Resolves the target list by scanning the filesystem under `skills/` and `.agents/skills/` for directories with both `SKILL.md` and `evals.json`. References the iteration-N workspace pattern and the per-skill `.workspace/` location. Plans to run BOTH the without_skill (RED) and with_skill (GREEN) variants per scenario, then grade deterministically.

assertions.text: scans the filesystem for skills with evals.json (mentions the dir or the file as the discovery mechanism)
names the per-skill workspace + iteration-N convention
names both variants (without_skill / RED and with_skill / GREEN) — not just one

evals.prompt: rerun the evals for ts-best-practices and tell me if anything regressed
evals.expected_output: Plans both the without_skill (RED baseline) and with_skill (GREEN) dispatches via the Agent tool — not just one. Saves transcripts to the per-skill `.workspace/iteration-N/eval-K-name/` location with the next iteration number computed from existing iterations. Grades each transcript deterministically (regex / contains / file_exists, no LLM-as-judge). Reports per-eval pass/fail and flags regressions explicitly.

assertions.text: dispatches BOTH without_skill (RED baseline) and with_skill (GREEN) — explicitly mentions both
names the deterministic grading mechanism (regex / contains / file_exists; no LLM-as-judge)
computes the next iteration number from existing workspace state (not 'iteration-1' blindly)
